原文: Diets have changed① in China—and so too has its top crop②. Since 2011, the country has grown more corn③ than rice. Corn production has jumped④ nearly 125 percent over the past 25 years, while rice has increased only 7 percent.
翻译: 中国的饮食结构已经发生了变化——其最主要的农作物也是如此。自2011年以来，中国种植的玉米比水稻更多。在过去25年里，玉米产量飙升了近125%，而水稻产量仅增长了7%。

① change /tʃeɪndʒ/ v. 改变 n. 变化
Ⓡ exchange /ɪksˈtʃeɪndʒ/ v. 交换 n. 交流
Ⓡ charge /tʃɑːdʒ/ v. 收费；充电 n. 费用
Ⓡ challenge /ˈtʃælɪndʒ/ n. 挑战 v. 质疑
② crop /krɒp/ n. 农作物；收成 v. 收割
Ⓡ harvest /ˈhɑːvɪst/ n. 收获 v. 收割
Ⓡ agriculture /ˈæɡrɪkʌltʃə(r)/ n. 农业
③ corn /kɔːn/ n. 玉米；谷物
Ⓡ grain /ɡreɪn/ n. 谷物；颗粒
Ⓡ wheat /wiːt/ n. 小麦
④ jump /dʒʌmp/ v. 跳跃；激增 n. 跳跃
Ⓡ increase /ɪnˈkriːs/ v. 增加 /ˈɪnkriːs/ n. 增长
Ⓡ rise /raɪz/ v. 上升 n. 上升
Ⓡ surge /sɜːdʒ/ v. 激增 n. 激增

原文: A taste⑤ for meat is actually behind the change: An important part of its corn is used to feed⑥ chickens, pigs, and cattle⑦. Another reason for corn's rise: The government encourages⑧ farmers to grow corn instead of rice to improve⑨ water quality. Corn uses less water than rice and creates less fertilizer⑩(化肥)run-off. This switch⑪ has decreased⑫ pollution in the country's major lakes and reservoirs⑬ and made drinking water safer for people.
翻译: 这种变化实际上源于对肉类的需求：其玉米的重要部分被用来喂养鸡、猪和牛。玉米崛起的另一个原因是：政府鼓励农民种植玉米而非水稻，以改善水质。玉米比水稻用水更少，产生的化肥径流也更少。这种转变减少了中国主要湖泊和水库的污染，并使饮用水对人们来说更安全。

原文: According to the World Bank, China accounts for⑭ about 30 percent of total global fertilizer consumption⑮. The Chinese Ministry of Agriculture finds that between 2005—when the government started a soil-testing program that/which gives specific⑯ fertilizer recommendations⑰ to farmers—and 2011, fertilizer use dropped by 7.7 million tons. That prevented the emission⑱(排放)of 51.8 million tons of carbon dioxide. China's approach⑲ to protecting its environment while feeding its citizens "offers useful lessons for agriculture and food policymakers worldwide,"says the bank's Juergen Voegele.
翻译: 根据世界银行的数据，中国约占全球化肥总消费量的30%。中国农业部发现，在2005年（当时政府启动了一项为农民提供具体化肥建议的土壤检测计划）到2011年之间，化肥使用量下降了770万吨。这防止了5180万吨二氧化碳的排放。世界银行的尤尔根·沃格勒表示，中国在养活其公民的同时保护环境的做法“为全球农业和食品政策制定者提供了有益的经验”。
